summary: In this clinical study abstract, Tamara B. Harris and colleagues demonstrate that elevated levels of interleukin-6 (IL-6) and C-reactive protein (CRP) are independent predictors of mortality in healthy, nondisabled older adults. The research suggests that measuring these inflammatory markers can help identify high-risk subgroups who might benefit from targeted anti-inflammatory interventions.

principles.statement: Elevated levels of interleukin-6 and C-reactive protein independently predict mortality in healthy older adults.
principles.rationale: Prospective population-based data shows that higher levels of these inflammatory markers, particularly when jointly elevated, significantly increase the risk of death regardless of age, sex, BMI, smoking history, or prior cardiovascular disease.
principles.source_quote: Higher circulating levels of interleukin-6 and C-reactive protein were associated with mortality in this population-based sample of healthy older persons.

principles.statement: Inflammatory biomarkers should be used to identify high-risk subgroups for targeted interventions.
principles.rationale: Because markers like IL-6 and CRP reliably predict mortality in otherwise healthy and nondisabled elderly populations, they provide a practical clinical tool for isolating individuals who would benefit most from anti-inflammatory treatments.
principles.source_quote: These measures may be useful for identification of high-risk subgroups for anti-inflammatory interventions.

signature_quotes.text: Higher circulating levels of interleukin-6 and C-reactive protein were associated with mortality in this population-based sample of healthy older persons. These measures may be useful for identification of high-risk subgroups for anti-inflammatory interventions.
signature_quotes.context: Concluding the findings of the Iowa 65+ Rural Health Study regarding inflammatory markers and mortality.

signature_quotes.text: Subjects with elevation of both interleukin-6 and C-reactive protein levels were 2.6 times more likely... to die during follow-up than those with low levels of both measurements.
signature_quotes.context: Describing the compounded mortality risk of having multiple elevated inflammatory markers.
